claim: Chapter 16 can frame data-centre power as a small global share but fast-growing and locally concentrated load class.
allowed_language: IEA-attributed global share/growth language and EPRI-attributed local concentration language.
blocked_language: Do not imply local insignificance from global share or convert concentration into named-project siting claims.
notes: Maps to CH16Q-001, CH16Q-002, CH16Q-009, and CH16Q-010.

claim: Chapter 16 can use LBNL/DOE U.S. 2023 estimates and 2028 scenarios as a quantitative spine only with measured-versus-scenario labels preserved.
allowed_language: Reported 2023 TWh/share and modeled 2028 scenario range with N16-1 mapping.
blocked_language: Do not collapse scenario ranges into a midpoint or write projections as happened history.

claim: Chapter 16 can explain interconnection as a timing constraint using EPRI facility-scale evidence and DOE-SEAB advisory lead-time/request language.
allowed_language: EPRI-attributed 100-1000 MW facility-scale range and DOE-SEAB-attributed 300-1000 MW requests with one-to-three-year lead-time pressure.
blocked_language: Do not treat advisory evidence as a complete interconnection database or proof of delay for a named campus.
notes: Maps to CH16Q-007, CH16Q-008, CH16Q-013, and CH16Q-014.

claim: Chapter 16 can separate corporate clean-power procurement from physical hourly electricity supply.
allowed_language: Procurement, certificates, PPAs, grid fuel mix, backup, storage, and flexibility may be discussed as separate layers.
blocked_language: Do not equate clean-power contracts with delivered electrons at a specific site and hour.

claim: Chapter 16 can treat cooling and rack density as facility constraints using DOE-SEAB response categories and Uptime operator-survey caution.
allowed_language: Facility-level cooling/power/water response categories and PUE/rack-density operator-survey signals.
blocked_language: Do not make detailed cooling-engineering claims or universal rack-density laws without later corroboration.

claim: Chapter 16 can use AI-factory language only as company framing and must keep energy-per-token estimates blocked.
allowed_language: Mechanism-stack language from electricity through tokens; NVIDIA/GTC as attributed company framing.
blocked_language: Do not validate NVIDIA performance/roadmap/deployment claims or quantify Wh/token from the current evidence pack.
